differential_diagnoses: Rheumatoid arthritis with secondary vasculitic neuropathy
next_steps: Monitor response to vitamin B6 supplementation
Rheumatology consultation for evaluation of symmetric polyarthritis and potential vasculitis
Serum inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP) and rheumatoid factor / anti-CCP antibody testing
Perform lumbar puncture for CSF analysis (protein, cytology, oligoclonal bands) to exclude paraneoplastic or inflammatory etiologies
Repeat high-quality MRI of the brain and entire spine with contrast to evaluate for occult vasculitis or spinal pathology
Consider nerve and muscle biopsy if clinical suspicion for vasculitis persists despite B6 repletion

differential_diagnoses: Late-onset rheumatoid arthritis with associated systemic vasculitic neuropathy
Systemic vasculitis (e.g., Polyarteritis nodosa or microscopic polyangiitis)
Paraneoplastic polyneuropathy with associated inflammatory arthritis
Multiple myeloma with secondary systemic amyloidosis
Vitamin B6 deficiency (likely multifactorial/secondary to malnutrition)
next_steps: Obtain inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP) and rheumatoid workup (RF, anti-CCP, ANA)
Perform serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) and immunofixation to rule out monoclonal gammopathy
Screen for cryoglobulins and order complement levels (C3, C4)
Perform high-quality MRI of the brain and entire spine with gadolinium contrast
Conduct a formal cognitive evaluation (e.g., MoCA) once delirium clears to differentiate from baseline dementia
Nerve and muscle biopsy if inflammatory markers are high and peripheral neuropathy persists
Request rheumatology and neurology consultations to coordinate multisystem investigation
